summary: A student fee payment app with UPI QR code integration. The student side has a payment QR feature being improved for better UPI compatibility and scannability.
architectural_notes: UPI QR codes must use the format upi://pay?pa=UPI_ID&pn=NAME&am=AMOUNT&cu=INR for broad app compatibility (Google Pay, PhonePe, BHIM, etc.)
known_issues: Student-side UPI payment QR code is not scannable by most UPI apps (e.g., Paytm, Google Pay) — likely due to incorrect QR format or encoding

existing_features.title: On the student-side payment page (frontend/src/pages/student/Book.tsx and any related payment QR rendering component), generate the QR code using a properly formatted UPI deep link in the format: `upi://pay?pa=<UPI_ID>&pn=<PAYEE_NAME>&am=<AMOUNT>&cu=INR`. Ensure this format is used consistently so all UPI-compliant apps (Google Pay, PhonePe, BHIM, Paytm, etc.) can scan and initiate payment without issues.

Upgrade the QR code rendering on the student payment page to use error correction level 'H' (highest, ~30% damage tolerance). Ensure the QR is rendered with sufficient quiet zone (at least 4 modules of white border), high contrast (black modules on white background), and at a resolution high enough for crisp display (minimum 256x256 logical pixels). If using a QR library like 'qrcode.react', set the appropriate props (errorCorrectionLevel='H', size, includeMargin=true).

Below the QR code on the student payment page, add a copyable fallback section that displays: (1) the UPI ID in a styled text field with a one-click copy-to-clipboard button, (2) the full UPI payment deep link as a tappable anchor that opens the user's default UPI app when clicked. Show a brief confirmation toast or inline feedback (e.g., 'Copied!') when the UPI ID is copied. Label this section clearly, e.g., 'Can't scan? Use these instead:'.

new_feature_requests.summary: Improve UPI payment QR code on student side: proper upi:// deep link format, high error correction (level H), and copyable UPI ID fallback
